title: Russia Signals Rejection of Ukraine's 20-Point Peace Counterproposal
detail: Russia's Kremlin foreign policy adviser Yuri Ushakov signals that Moscow expects to formally reject Ukraine's 20-point peace counterproposal, describing it as incompatible with Russian negotiating positions and 'disconnected from military realities.' The counterproposal was Ukraine's response to U.S.-Russia back-channel communications, and sought security guarantees, international monitoring of any ceasefire line, and restoration of territorial integrity. U.S. special envoy Steve Witkoff announces representatives will travel to Islamabad on April 19 for a second round of talks with Iran — highlighting the Trump administration's simultaneous multipolar diplomatic agenda. Zelensky, excluded from the U.S.-Russia track, continues to demand direct Ukrainian participation. The Carnegie Endowment notes that three rounds of U.S.-Russia talks since January 2026 have not produced even a shared definition of what 'peace' would look like in Ukraine.
confidenceReason: Russia's rejection position confirmed via Kremlin-linked sources; full counterproposal text not publicly released

media.caption: Carnegie analysis: Three rounds of US-Russia talks have not produced shared peace framework for Ukraine
